rationale: Both correctly diagnose the run-level/dummy-weight root cause matching the thread's resolution (invalid, fixed by --run-level=full_model, tied to PR #4354) and cite the same core files. X goes further with more granular, multi-file grounding (runtime.py fixture logic, assertions.py thresholds, the pytest-marker-vs-run-level distinction) and addresses more of the reporter's concrete situation (hardware/A100 caveat, video path dependency, GPU memory split across stages) plus a forward-looking ergonomics suggestion. Y is correct but thinner, leaning on the same three citations without deeper verification, and its 'Run report' scaffolding reads as unpolished for a maintainer-style answer.
